data.title: Silvercorp Metals Inc. acquires a 70% interest in Tulkubash/Kyzyltash Gold Projects in Kyrgyzstan, signed Agreements with Chaarat Gold Holdings Limited and National Investment Agency. (Characters: 163)
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 20, 2026, Silvercorp Metals Inc. (TSX: SVM) announced the signing of a Share Purchase Agreement with Chaarat Gold Holdings Limited and a Cooperation Agreement with the National Investment Agency. This agreement is a significant milestone for Silvercorp, showcasing their commitment to strategic partnerships. The details of the agreements were not disclosed, but it indicates a positive development for Silvercorp as they continue to expand their business portfolio. Shareholders are optimistic about the future prospects of the Company following this announcement.

data.title: Phillips Edison & Company (Nasdaq: PECO) discloses tax reporting info for 2025 distributions to shareholders.
data.description: Announced on January 20, 2026 in Cincinnati, Phillips Edison & Company, Inc. (Nasdaq: PECO), a major owner and operator of premium grocery-anchored neighborhood shopping centers in the US, shared tax reporting details for the 2025 distributions to its common stockholders. PECO stands as one of the leading entities in this sector, and the company's recent tax reporting update sheds light on its financial transactions for the specified period.

data.title: Glass House Brands accelerates its 2026 expansion plans due to success with past development, focusing on capitalizing on cannabis reform opportunities.
data.description: Glass House Brands Inc., a leading cannabis company, has expedited its expansion plans to 2026 due to successful prior developments and in anticipation of upcoming cannabis reforms and potential business prospects. The company, headquartered in Long Beach, Calif., and Toronto, is listed as CBOE CA: GLAS.A.U, CBOE CA: GLAS.WT.U, OTCQX: GLASF, and OTCQX:GHBWF. The decision reflects its status as one of the fastest-growing vertically-integrated cannabis firms in the U.S. The move signifies a strategic response to market dynamics and favorable outcomes, highlighting the company's aggressive growth trajectory and readiness to seize emerging opportunities.

data.title: Celcuity's NDA for gedatolisib in HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA wild-type ABC accepted by FDA for Priority Review, PDUFA on July 17, 2026.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC) disclosed that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved the filing of its New Drug Application (NDA) for gedatolisib, targeting hormone receptor positive (HR+), HER2-negative, PIK3CA wild-type advanced breast cancer (ABC). The FDA designated it for Priority Review and set a Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) target date of July 17, 2026.

data.title: Medicus Pharma Ltd. marked one year on Nasdaq with AI drug development, multiple Phase 2 Catalysts, and SKNJCT-003 study progress.
data.description: As we move into 2026, the Company is equipped with numerous Phase 2 Catalysts and an AI-driven approach to drug development. Additionally, there is regulatory flexibility as we near the database lock, topline clinical data disclosure, and preparation for potential partnerships in relation to the SKNJCT-003 study.

data.title: Sustainable Development Capital LLP partners with FuelCell Energy to boost distributed generation deployment for data centers.
data.description: Boosting the installation of distributed generation through a top-notch project execution capability.

data.title: Willis Lease Finance Corporation declares $0.40 dividend per share, to be paid on Feb 20, 2026 to stockholders of record on Feb 11.
data.description: In Coconut Creek, Florida on January 20, 2026, Willis Lease Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: WLFC) shared that they are the primary lessor of commercial aircraft engines and a worldwide provider of aviation services. They revealed a quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share for the Company's common stock. The dividend is set to be distributed on February 20, 2026, to stockholders recorded as of the end of business on February 11, 2026.

data.title: KeepZone AI, a subsidiary of Jeffs' Brands, secures exclusive reseller deal for anti-drone systems in Mexico with Aerospace Company.
data.description: In Tel Aviv, Israel, on Jan. 20, 2026, Jeffs' Brands Ltd, a data-driven e-commerce company listed on Nasdaq under JFBR and JFBRW, is venturing into the global homeland security sector leveraging advanced artificial intelligence (AI) solutions. The company disclosed that its subsidiary, KeepZone AI Inc., has secured an exclusive reseller agreement with a top aerospace defense technology developer.

data.title: Soluna appoints Michael Picchi as CFO and Treasurer starting April 1, 2026 to lead finance and capital strategy.
data.description: Albany-based Soluna Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: SLNH) has named Michael Picchi as the new Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer starting April 1, 2026. Picchi will oversee the finance department as the company, specializing in eco-friendly data centers for high-tech operations like Bitcoin mining and AI, pursues its growth strategy. Soluna aims to enhance its renewable energy-powered data center concept and enter the Al market under Picchi's leadership.

data.title: Mantle and Everclear collaborate to introduce Cross-Chain Asset Settlement for efficient asset flow within ecosystem.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE, on Jan. 20, 2026, Mantle revealed a partnership with Everclear to bring Cross-Chain Asset Settlement. Mantle is a top-tier platform connecting traditional finance, real-world assets, and on-chain liquidity. This collaboration aims to enhance liquidity processes and asset distribution.

data.title: BHP Group raises copper outlook due to record pricing, despite 4% decline in Q2 production to 490.5 Kt. Iron ore production increased 5% to 69.7 Mt, but steelmaking coal fell 3% to 4.3 Mt. In first half of fiscal 2026, copper production stayed flat at 984.1 Kt, while iron ore output rose 2% to 133.8 Mt. CEO Mike Henry optimistic about future prospects. [Word count: 55]
data.description: BHP Group Limited's (NYSE: BHP) stock dipped following the release of its second-quarter and first-half fiscal 2026 results. The company reported a 4% year-over-year decrease in copper production to 490.5 Kt, with Escondida mine's output falling by 7% and Pampa Norte's by 13% in the quarter. Quarterly copper production also saw a 1% decrease due to lower grades at Escondida. On the other hand, iron ore production grew by 5% year-over-year to 69.7 Mt, while steelmaking coal production dropped by 3% to 4.3 Mt. Sequentially, iron ore production surged by 9% driven by increased output at WAIO. In the first half of fiscal 2026, copper production totaled 984.1 Kt, showing no change from the previous year, and iron ore output increased by 2% year-over-year to reach 133.8 Mt. BHP's CEO, Mike Henry, commented, "BHP delivered another half of strong operational performance amidst challenging conditions."

data.title: Backlash against WLFI as top nine wallets hold 60% power in USD1 growth vote, raising concerns about insider influence.
data.description: Nearly 60% of the voting power in WLFI's governance vote was held by the top nine wallets, leading to concerns about insider influence. This discrepancy left locked holders, who were unable to participate, questioning the fairness of the process.

data.title: South Korea considers ending exclusive bank partnerships for crypto exchanges as part of assessing competition for Digital Asset Basic Act.
data.description: South Korea is currently evaluating exclusive bank partnerships for crypto exchanges as regulators examine competition and get ready for the implementation of the Digital Asset Basic Act.

data.title: Psyence BioMed delays reverse stock split effective date announcement, Nasdaq: PBM stock impacted.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Psyence Biomedical Ltd. (Nasdaq: PBM) disclosed the decision to delay the planned effective date of its reverse stock split for its common stock. The company, referred to as Psyence BioMed, opted to postpone this action affecting its issued and outstanding common stock shares.

data.title: The Sports Facilities Companies plans to open or start construction on 11 new venues nationwide in 2026, boosting youth sports and community recreation.
data.description: Clearwater, Florida - On January 20, 2026, The Sports Facilities Companies (SFC) revealed their plans for expanding access to youth sports, community recreation, and entertainment. They shared the news about opening or starting construction on a total of 11 new venues in various locations. This initiative aims to provide more opportunities for families and communities to engage in sports and recreational activities.

data.title: Catalyst Acoustics Group appoints C.M. as new CEO, effective Jan. 12.
data.description: A promotion was recently announced on January 20, 2026 by Catalyst Acoustics Group, a leading company in acoustic, seismic, vibration, and noise control brands. Charles Merrimon (C.M.) has been promoted to Chief Executive Officer as of January 12. The company is headquartered in Agawam, Massachusetts and is known for its elite group of brands focusing on these areas. This change in leadership signifies an important move for the company as it continues to grow and expand its operations.
